name: Velma Middleton
abstract: Velma Middleton (September 1, 1917 – February 10, 1961) was an American jazz vocalist and entertainer who sang with Louis Armstrong's big bands and small groups from 1942 until her death.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins' One-Night Stand: Louis Armstrong
Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Woody Herman and His Third Herd/ Louis Armstrong and His All Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: That was when we were going to Europe in 1948 with Louis Armstrong--he was with Louis, you know--and there were several bands going for that International Festival in Nice and Earl Hines and Jack Teagarden and Sid Catlett and Barney Bigard and Arvell Shaw and Velma Middleton, Louis, and...who was pl

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: I think that that was one of the best bands that Louie had, quintets, with Earl Hines, Jack Teagarden, Barney Bigard, Arvell Shaw, Louie and me. And Velma Middleton singing.

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: Velma Middleton was six. She was the vocalist. That's about it, six.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: Edmond Hall was on it with me when I went later on, and Barney Bigard and Velma Middleton. I think they fixed up about a six piece band and they went out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: So everybody on the train is gambling-- Velma Middleton, Humph. M. HILLIARD; Shelton Simmons Sonny Woods... Louis Russell was in it, too.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: we'd perform with Velma Middleton, and stayed there, we worked there for a space of about three weeks
